metadata.title: Talazoparib Therapy
metadata.indication: Monotherapy for adults with germline BRCA1/2-mutated, HER2-negative locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer following appropriate prior anthracycline/taxane and endocrine therapy where relevant.

clinical_governance.prescriptive_authority: Treatment plan must be initiated by a Consultant Medical Oncologist.
clinical_governance.dose_modification_note: Any dose modification should be discussed with a Consultant.
clinical_governance.disclaimer: Decision-support encoding of the NCCP source. It does not replace the current NCCP regimen, clinical judgement, local policy, prescribing review or pharmacy verification.

indications.description: Talazoparib monotherapy for germline BRCA1/2-mutated HER2-negative locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer.

administration.administration_note: Continuous once daily until progression or unacceptable toxicity. Do not replace a vomited or missed dose.

symptomatic_cns_or_leptomeningeal.label: Symptomatic CNS metastases or leptomeningeal disease

output_templates.proceed: No entered value triggered an encoded talazoparib hold or modification rule. Complete normal prescribing and pharmacy checks.
output_templates.coverage_gap: Only entered values were assessed; unentered clinically relevant fields remain unassessed.

action.recommendation: Withhold talazoparib. Resume only when Hb ≥9 g/dL and at the next lower dose level.

rules.explanation: Withhold talazoparib. Resume only when Hb ≥9 g/dL and at the next lower dose level.

action.recommendation: Withhold talazoparib. Resume only when ANC ≥1.5 ×10⁹/L and at the next lower dose level.

rules.explanation: Withhold talazoparib. Resume only when ANC ≥1.5 ×10⁹/L and at the next lower dose level.

action.recommendation: Withhold talazoparib. Resume only when platelets ≥75 ×10⁹/L and at the next lower dose level.

rules.explanation: Withhold talazoparib. Resume only when platelets ≥75 ×10⁹/L and at the next lower dose level.

action.recommendation: Creatinine clearance 30–59 mL/min: recommended starting dose is 0.75 mg once daily.

rules.explanation: Creatinine clearance 30–59 mL/min: recommended starting dose is 0.75 mg once daily.

action.recommendation: Creatinine clearance 15–29 mL/min: recommended starting dose is 0.5 mg once daily.

rules.explanation: Creatinine clearance 15–29 mL/min: recommended starting dose is 0.5 mg once daily.

action.recommendation: CrCl <15 mL/min or haemodialysis: talazoparib has not been studied; no dosing recommendation is available.

rules.explanation: CrCl <15 mL/min or haemodialysis: talazoparib has not been studied; no dosing recommendation is available.

action.recommendation: Withhold for grade 3–4 non-haematological toxicity until recovery to grade ≤1; consider resuming at the next lower dose or discontinue.

rules.explanation: Withhold for grade 3–4 non-haematological toxicity until recovery to grade ≤1; consider resuming at the next lower dose or discontinue.

action.recommendation: Avoid strong P-gp inhibitors. If unavoidable, reduce talazoparib to the next lower dose and reassess after the inhibitor is stopped.

rules.explanation: Avoid strong P-gp inhibitors. If unavoidable, reduce talazoparib to the next lower dose and reassess after the inhibitor is stopped.

action.recommendation: Suspected MDS/AML or persistent unexplained cytopenia: withhold and investigate, including haematology review as appropriate.

rules.explanation: Suspected MDS/AML or persistent unexplained cytopenia: withhold and investigate, including haematology review as appropriate.

action.recommendation: Prior PARP inhibitor treatment is listed as an NCCP exclusion.

rules.explanation: Prior PARP inhibitor treatment is listed as an NCCP exclusion.

action.recommendation: Symptomatic CNS metastases or leptomeningeal disease is listed as an NCCP exclusion.

rules.explanation: Symptomatic CNS metastases or leptomeningeal disease is listed as an NCCP exclusion.

action.recommendation: Breast-feeding is an NCCP exclusion during talazoparib treatment.

rules.explanation: Breast-feeding is an NCCP exclusion during talazoparib treatment.

summary: No routine premedication specified by NCCP.
Monitor FBC monthly and provide transfusion or growth-factor support when clinically appropriate.
